steps.title: Identify the structure in the blank
steps.content: The blank must be a noun phrase serving as the subject of the clause have a special genetic ability. It consists of a noun (cephalopods) followed by an appositive phrase that elaborates on it.

steps.title: Apply the rule for appositives
steps.content: A non-restrictive appositive phrase must be enclosed with matching punctuation on both sides. Since the appositive contains internal commas, em dashes are preferred to commas for clarity. Choice D correctly uses matching em dashes to set off the entire appositive phrase, with no trailing punctuation because have follows directly. Choices A and B either leave the appositive unclosed or split it incorrectly, and C misplaces a colon. That matches choice D — D is correct.
